question.text: In the FFSL case, the total amount raised through both preferential allotments was approximately Rs. 15 crores. Out of this, Rs. 6.57 crore was transferred back to allottees indirectly and Rs. 7.94 crore was transferred to entities connected to FFSL. What approximate percentage of the total allotment proceeds was transferred to entities connected to FFSL?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Rs. 7.94 crore transferred to connected entities out of Rs. 15 crore total = (7.94/15) x 100 = 52.93%, approximately 52.9%. **The Trap:** 43.8% would be the percentage transferred back to allottees (6.57/15 = 43.8%). The question specifically asks about transfers to CONNECTED ENTITIES of FFSL, not back to allottees. **Cross-Exam Context:** Calculation questions involving fund flow analysis are tested in NISM Series III-A and IX. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** In fraud investigations, tracing fund flows is critical - nearly 97% of the Rs. 15 crore (6.57 + 7.94 = 14.51 crore) did not serve the stated purpose of the allotment.
